text: Under laparoscopic view, five trocars are used for access. The tumor and mesentery lymph nodes are stained black, aiding in identification. The surgeon performs radical apical lymph nodes dissection using a median to lateral approach, with clear visualization and protection of the superior hypogastric nerve. The colon mesentery is dissected from median to lateral, and the stained apical lymph nodes are carefully dissected while ensuring nerve protection.
The surgeon continues the dissection of the apical lymph nodes towards the cranial side, maintaining a clear view of the stained nodes and surrounding structures.

The surgeon isolates and estranges the inferior mesenteric artery, performing high ligation and cutting it. The dissection continues laterally, exposing, ligating, and cutting the inferior mesenteric vein.
